note: The official baseline defines no fixed first-contact greeting.
Warm reduction of an overwhelmed request to one manageable step is not a default rule.
The default says to skip performative filler, help directly, and stay concise when appropriate.
Ordinary interpretation of typos and the exact safety-caveat shape remain model-dependent.
Proceeding under one brief reversible assumption is not specified.
The official baseline has no outcome-aware A/B/C plus free-form choice contract.
External caution is present, but there is no rule forbidding inferred or default Reminder times.
The baseline asks before uncertain external action, but does not bind exact date, time, zone, list, and count to a separate preview revision.
A concise non-defensive repair shape is not specified.
Sarcasm and idiom handling remain model-dependent.
Brief emotional acknowledgement without diagnosis or cognitive overload is not specified.
The template does not define attachment, exclusivity, or dependency boundaries.
OpenClaw's short safety reminder is not a health-response contract.
OpenClaw's short safety reminder is not a legal uncertainty and qualified-help contract.
Gentle disagreement and financial-risk framing are not specified by the OpenClaw default.
The baseline explicitly says to be resourceful before asking and return answers rather than unnecessary questions.
Privacy is explicit, but data minimization and avoiding unnecessary sensitive-detail extraction are not fully defined.
Sessions and long-running work have state, but the default does not require a one-sentence non-destructive topic-switch notice.
Command and steering queues exist, but additive rapid-message merging is not a conversational default contract.
External caution aligns, but edited payload invalidation and mandatory new confirmation revision are not specified.
The baseline advises asking before external action, but does not define the exact action-time confirmation split.
Approval mechanisms exist, but the benefit, exact scope, official prompt location, and decline impact are not a fixed message contract.
The official personal-agent pack has a denial scenario that must stop without tool use or fake progress.
Secret leakage is discouraged, but routing credentials only to an official secure field is not fully specified.
Long-running execution guidance and no-fake-progress checks exist, but the user-facing event-driven start template is not fixed.
There is no default user-facing cadence for unchanged long-running work.
The official personal-agent pack explicitly requires proof-backed completion rather than fake progress.
Retry machinery exists, but duplicate-effect suppression plus an existing Receipt response is not a default conversational contract.
The failure-recovery scenario requires honest failure status, no false completion, and a clear retry boundary.
No-fake-progress and retry boundaries align, but an ambiguous-effect result is not given this exact no-retry message rule.
The failure-recovery and task-status scenarios separate completed, failed or blocked, and next-step state.
OpenClaw tests for leaked backend failures, but deterministic safe copy and preserved user state are not fully specified here.
Gateway and background runtime state exist, but the exact Off copy and no-model/no-action guarantee are OpenOpen-specific.
File-backed continuity is explicit, but interrupted effects and evidence-only recovery wording are not a default conversational rule.
Return behavior under thirty minutes is not defined.
A one-sentence recap after a medium absence is not defined.
Evidence-based return recaps after a day or state change are not defined.
External caution aligns, but pending previews, silence, freshness, and no-nag behavior are not fully specified.
Cancellation acknowledgement and retained-local-state wording are not defined.
OpenClaw recommends capturing preferences in long-term memory; OpenOpen defaults inferred style preferences to session-only until explicit confirmation.
File-backed memory is inspectable, but immediate preference rollback and a no-argument correction dialogue are not defined.
Density may adapt through the model and user files, but transient expertise inference and non-persistence are not specified.
The default does not require one common-language setup action per instruction for non-developers.
iMessage capability and approvals exist, but the exact one-step same-account self-chat setup and exclusions are not the generic default.
Channel isolation and privacy guidance align, but selected-self-chat-only ingestion and reply denial are an OpenOpen product boundary.
Opinionated personality and humor are encouraged, but rare dry humor only after the useful answer is not fixed by the default.
Limited-English interpretation without correction remains model-dependent.
Queued message handling exists, but merging dictated fragments and corrections while preserving order is not a fixed response contract.
The official baseline has no outcome-aware A/B/C plus free-form family-scheduling route.
The official baseline has no structured choice rule for conflicting caregiving goals or its emotional-load constraints.
